one_time_description: Prior period adjustment recognizing previously unrecorded RBC litigation loans for Timber claim and Boundary claim costs incurred prior to April 1, 2021
economic_notes: First Nation holds 51% interest in Bagone'an JS Drilling Inc. and Eshkaa NiiBiish-Day Inc. through 1988183 Ontario Limited. Chi-Zhiingwaak Business Park Corporation is a consolidated subsidiary. Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership distributions restricted to community development, health, education, cultural and economic development. Original Traders Energy LP lease for three lots in business park commenced September 2021. Solar park asset on balance sheet.
notes: Fiscal year is 2023 (year ended March 31, 2023). Prior period adjustment of $1,274,284 recorded to recognize previously unbooked RBC litigation loans (Timber claim $1,132,157 and Boundary claim $576,757 less adjustments). Long-term debt includes FNFA debt of $3,338,656 and RBC/CMHC debt of $5,378,281 totalling $8,716,937. Two GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: Bagone'an JS Drilling Inc. (51%) and Eshkaa NiiBiish-Day Inc. (51%). Two consolidated subsidiaries: 1988183 Ontario Limited and Chi-Zhiingwaak Business Park Corporation. Revenue from 'Other' includes Ontario First Nations LP distributions and own-source revenues. Total government transfers may include provincial OFNLP gaming revenues embedded in 'Other' revenue line but cannot be separately quantified; only ISC amount is clearly identified. OSR calculated as total revenue minus ISC funding only. Schedule of remuneration not included in provided document pages. Annual debt service represents principal repayments on long-term debt only ($160,917 CMHC/TD mortgages + $167,356 FNFA = $328,273); RBC litigation loans were interest-only.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
